events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective"],
  "percent_complete": 70,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.9,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Bearing inner race shows bluing/discoloration indicating overheating during installation — excessive interference fit or improper mounting force",
    "Thermal damage to bearing steel compromises hardness and fatigue life; bearing is installed-defective and must be replaced",
    "Bluing on ID suggests heat was generated by improper pressing technique or lack of thermal expansion control during mounting"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Remove and replace overheated bearing — do not proceed to closeout",
    "Inspect shaft journal and housing for associated heat damage or material transfer",
    "Verify correct shaft tolerance and bearing fit class against SKF/Timken mounting guide specifications",
    "Use proper mounting procedure: temperature-controlled heating (induction heater or oil bath) for interference fits, or sleeve and hydraulic press for cold mounting",
    "Document root cause and retrain installer on correct bearing mounting technique"
  ],
  "rationale": "Bluing on the bearing ID is a definitive indicator of thermal damage from overheating during installation. This alters the metallurgical properties of the bearing steel, reducing hardness and fatigue life. Per SKF and Timken mounting guides, bearings must not be subjected to temperatures exceeding approximately 120°C (250°F) during installation, and proper mounting methods must be used to avoid excessive force or friction heat. The bearing is installed but defective and must be rejected at this quality gate.",
  "references": [
    "SKF Bearing Installation and Maintenance Guide — mounting methods and temperature limits",
    "Timken Bearing Installation Guide — interference fit mounting procedures and thermal damage prevention"
  ]
}
